explanation: The architecture diagram shows only two primary business services, Service-A and Service-B. The other components are standard infrastructure services (API Gateway, Service Discovery, Config Server, etc.) that support the microservice ecosystem. The Nano Service smell relates to business logic being decomposed into excessively fine-grained units. Without any information on the specific responsibilities of Service-A and Service-B, it is impossible to definitively assess their granularity. However, in a system with only two business services, it is highly unlikely that the decomposition has gone too far. The smell is typically found in systems with a much larger number of services. Therefore, the presence of the Nano Service smell is considered unlikely.
